authored.para: This instruction calculates an address from a base register value and an optional offset, and stores two 32-bit words or two 64-bit doublewords to the calculated address, from two registers. For information on single-copy atomicity and alignment requirements, see Requirements for single-copy atomicity and Alignment of data accesses. The instruction also has memory ordering semantics, as described in Load-Acquire, Load-AcquirePC, and Store-Release, with the additional requirement that:

listitem.content: When using the pre-index addressing mode, the Memory effects associated with Xt2/Wt2 are Ordered-before the Memory effects associated with Xt1/Wt1.
For all other addressing modes, the Memory effects associated with Xt1/Wt1 are Ordered-before the Memory effects associated with Xt2/Wt2.

authored.para: For information about addressing modes, see Load/Store addressing modes.

encodingnotes.para: STILP has the same CONSTRAINED UNPREDICTABLE behavior as STP. For information about this CONSTRAINED UNPREDICTABLE behavior, see Architectural Constraints on UNPREDICTABLE behaviors, and particularly STP and STILP.

operationalnote_content.para: This instruction is a data-independent-time instruction as described in About PSTATE.DIT.

a.hover: Is the 32-bit name of the first general-purpose register to be transferred, encoded in the "Rt" field.
Is the 32-bit name of the second general-purpose register to be transferred, encoded in the "Rt2" field.
Is the 64-bit name of the general-purpose base register or stack pointer, encoded in the "Rn" field.
Is the 32-bit name of the first general-purpose register to be transferred, encoded in the "Rt" field.
Is the 32-bit name of the second general-purpose register to be transferred, encoded in the "Rt2" field.
Is the 64-bit name of the general-purpose base register or stack pointer, encoded in the "Rn" field.
Is the 64-bit name of the first general-purpose register to be transferred, encoded in the "Rt" field.
Is the 64-bit name of the second general-purpose register to be transferred, encoded in the "Rt2" field.
Is the 64-bit name of the general-purpose base register or stack pointer, encoded in the "Rn" field.
Is the 64-bit name of the first general-purpose register to be transferred, encoded in the "Rt" field.
Is the 64-bit name of the second general-purpose register to be transferred, encoded in the "Rt2" field.
Is the 64-bit name of the general-purpose base register or stack pointer, encoded in the "Rn" field.

ps.name: A64.ldst.ldiappstilp.STILP_32SE_ldiappstilp

intro.para: Is the 32-bit name of the first general-purpose register to be transferred, encoded in the "Rt" field.
Is the 32-bit name of the second general-purpose register to be transferred, encoded in the "Rt2" field.
Is the 64-bit name of the general-purpose base register or stack pointer, encoded in the "Rn" field.
Is the 64-bit name of the first general-purpose register to be transferred, encoded in the "Rt" field.
Is the 64-bit name of the second general-purpose register to be transferred, encoded in the "Rt2" field.

ps.name: A64.ldst.ldiappstilp.STILP_32SE_ldiappstilp
